decisions.content: PreToolUse:Bash can intercept and block destructive commands via exit 2
PreToolUse:Edit/Write can intercept file modifications and block via exit 2
PostToolUse hooks cannot block (exit 2 ignored), only observe
Hook stdin is JSON with tool_input.command (Bash) or tool_input.file_path (Edit/Write)
Multiple matching hooks run in parallel; deny > defer > ask > allow
Plugin hooks use CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT for portable paths
Plugin scope is below project/user/managed in priority
CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is replaced on update (managed by Claude Code)
deny rules are bypassable via indirect execution (bash -c)
Hooks are not bypassable by the agent (primary enforcement)
Hook failure default is non-blocking (fail-open, PD-71)
jq is required for JSON parsing in hook scripts
macOS lacks grep -oP (Perl regex); POSIX alternatives required
Plugin must work on macOS (BSD) and Linux (GNU) without modification
Destructive commands detectable by regex (rm -rf, git push --force, etc.)
Secret files detectable by filename patterns (*.env, *.pem, *.key)
Test tampering detectable by disable-keywords in test file paths
Blocked events must be logged from PreToolUse (PostToolUse never fires for blocked tools)
